title: Marbella Speedboat Charter: Costs, Season & Best Boats 2026
html:
Chartering a speedboat in Marbella offers a quick escape to the Mediterranean, with departures from Puerto Banús and prices starting around €400 for a half-day hire. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Marbella or list your own.

How much does it cost to rent a speedboat in Marbella?

A half-day speedboat charter in Marbella typically costs between €400 and €1,200, depending on the boat and season. For example, a shared tour on a RIB may start at €80 per person, while a private Maiora 26m can exceed €2,500 per day. Always check the exact price and inclusions when you compare verified boat listings via boat hire Marbella.

Do you need a licence to charter a speedboat in Marbella?

Yes, Spanish law requires a valid boat licence for any motorboat over 15 horsepower, but the operator can confirm exact requirements. If you lack a licence, you can hire a skipper or book a crewed charter. The local seamen prefer the afternoon levante wind, which can freshen the sea by 3pm.

Ask your charter company about the ‘llaut’ boats, traditional wooden vessels that double as stylish speedboats in Marbella harbour.

Where to set off in Marbella

Most speedboat charters depart from Puerto Banús, the glamorous marina lined with designer boutiques and restaurants. Other popular departure points include the Real Club Náutico de Marbella, Puerto de la Bajadilla (the old fishing port), and the nearby marina at Cabopino, which is quieter. Many trips head to the Cabo de Calaburras lighthouse or continue east to the Calahonda beaches. For a longer excursion, you can motor 8 nautical miles to the island of Isla de las Palomas (Alborán Sea), though this is often part of a full-day charter.

Which boat to rent

  1. RIB speedboat, best for thrill-seeking and watersports; up to 8 guests; from €400 half-day.
  2. Azimut 42, ideal for a luxury day cruise; 10 guests; from €1,200 half-day.
  3. Axopar 37, versatile hybrid for exploring coves; 10 guests; from €800 half-day.
  4. Sunseeker 50, suitable for entertaining; 12 guests; from €2,000 half-day.
  5. Maiora 26m, a full-crewed superyacht for large groups; 18 guests; from €5,000 full-day; see the Maiora 26m listing for details.

Cost breakdown for Marbella speedboat charter 2026

  • Shared speedboat tour (RIB, 2 hours): €80, €150 per person.
  • Small motorboat half-day (without skipper, up to 6 guests): €300, €600.
  • Catamaran full-day (with skipper, up to 12 guests): €1,500, €3,000.
  • Crewed luxury yacht full-day (accommodation optional, up to 12 guests): €3,000, €8,000.

At a glance

  • Price band: €80, €8,000 depending on boat and duration.
  • Peak months: June to September (high season).
  • Trip length: 2 to 8 hours, plus overnight options.
  • Capacity: 6 to 18 guests per boat.
  • Currency: Euro (EUR).

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Can I charter a speedboat without a licence in Marbella?

Yes, you can book a crewed charter where the owner supplies a skipper, so no licence is needed.

What is the best time of year for a speedboat charter in Marbella?

The best time is between June and September, when the sea is calm and the temperature reaches 30°C.

How far is it from Puerto Banús to the nearest island?

Isla de las Palomas is about 8 nautical miles south-west of Puerto Banús, a 30-minute run in a speedboat.

Can I take the boat to the beach resturants along the coast?

Many charter operators allow you to stop at chiringuitos (beach bars) such as Trocadero Arena or La Pesquera for lunch.

Closing remarks

Whether you choose a nimble Axopar or a stately Maiora, a speedboat charter in Marbella gives you the freedom to explore the Costa del Sol from the water. If you own a boat, consider listing it on BoatHire24 to reach thousands of travellers planning their 2026 holiday.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
